observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) henceforth to direct the Municipal Health Office to report all COVID-19 related cases to DOH for transparency and monitoring purposes.
We recommended that Management submit a copy of duly submitted reports to DOH that contains all COVID related patients handled by the Rural Health Unit (RHU) or quarantine facility, if any.
We recommended that the Management prepare the SRA payroll in accordance with Section 2.e of AO No. 36 and refund the said amount due to improper computation.

We recommended that Management provide a TOR, or in the contract with each consultant the specific nature of work assignments and/or specific duties and responsibilities, quantity of work to be accomplished and the desired work outputs as well as the reporting requirements and working time to facilitate comparison and evaluation of actual work accomplishments of hired consultants.
We recommended that the Municipality refrain from entering into contract prior to the issuance of BAC Resolution, as BAC Resolution form as an integral part of the contract.
We recommended that the Municipality refrain from initiation of contracts prior to the issuance of NTP in compliance with Section 37.4.1 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Property and Accounting Units undertake the necessary reconciliation procedures as outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006 to establish the correct balance of the PPE and record the necessary adjustments.
Further, that the format for RPCPPE prescribed by MNGAS, Volume II, be used and a summary per type of PPE be prepared thereof for reconciliation purposes with the Accounting records and that the same shall be certified correct by all members of the Inventory Committee.

We recommended that Management instruct the GSO to accomplish the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) for all the unserviceable properties subject for disposal.
Further, that the Appraisal and Disposal Committee conduct the appraisal of the unserviceable properties to determine the modes of disposal and the appraised values, if any, of the properties to be disposed to facilitate its immediate disposal in accordance with Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 89-296.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to hasten the preparation of the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements and provide the Audit Team with a copy thereof, including the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) for the recording of the adjustments/reconciling items, for review and verification.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the unsubmitted Disbursement Vouchers for the proper recording in the books of accounts and for evaluation by the Audit Team as to the propriety and validity of the transactions. Henceforth, for the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the timeline in the submission of accounts and reports to the Office of the Municipal Accountant which is on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month.

In view of the foregoing observation, we recommended to Management the following:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to conduct immediate reconciliation of the individual balances of the Cash-in Local Treasury account with the pertinent Cashbooks and make the necessary adjustments thereon; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the reconciling items in the books of accounts based on the BRS prepared. Moreover, furnish the Municipal Treasurer copies of the BRS for the recording of reconciling items, if any, pertaining to its records.
We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer maintain Cashbook-Cash in Bank by fund and by account number to facilitate reconciliation of the treasury and accounting records.
We recommended that the agency determine the accountabilities of AOs based on the average monthly disbursement in a year to ensure that the AOs are sufficiently covered with bond.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a request for authority to write-off the dormant receivable accounts amounting to ₱1,099,345.54 following the procedures in Sections 8.2 and 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management submit the above-mentioned documentary requirements in support of the payment for terminal leave benefits.
We recommended that henceforth Management support all transactions with valid, complete and proper documents as required under Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

In line with the foregoing observation, we recommended to Management the following:
Require the end-user/implementing units to prepare the PPMPs in accordance with Section 7.1 of the RIRR of RA 9184 and make sure that only those considered crucial to the efficient discharge of government functions shall be included; and
Require the Municipal Budget Office to thoroughly evaluate the submitted PPMPs in coherence with the proposed budget.

We recommended that the agency require the end-user/implementing units to fill-up all the information required in the preparation of the PPMP under Section 7.3.2 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 and submit the same on a timely manner.
We recommended that the LCE henceforth direct the BAC to utilize the GPPB approved format of the APP.

We recommended that the agency prepare its APCPI and immediately submit a copy to GPPB TSO. Whereas, APCPI results are among the required documents to be submitted to GPPB for the grant of Performance-Based Bonus every year starting CY 2015.

In line with the foregoing observation, we recommended to Management the following:
Submit through electronic mail a copy of its APP and APCPI results to the GPPB, in both Microsoft Excel and PDF File; and
Post the APP and PMR in the agency website or in a conspicuous place reserved for this purpose in the premises of the Municipality and submit a certification indicating that it has complied with either of the said posting requirements to the GPPB.
We recommended that the LCE direct the BAC to keep abreast of the latest promulgation of the GPPB regarding the standard forms need to be submitted by the prospective bidders.

We recommended that the agency conduct the pre-bid conference twelve (12) calendar days before the deadline for the submission and receipt of bids, but not earlier than seven (7) calendar days from the PhilGEPS posting of the Invitation to Bid (ITB) or bidding documents in accordance with Section 22.2 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipality impose liquidated damages corresponding to the number of days delayed pursuant to Section 68, Rule XXII of the RIRR of RA 9184, if no justifiable reason was communicated by the supplier and approved by the LCE.

We recommended that Management:
Submit the lacking required documents; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure submission of complete required documents in the procurement of goods.

We recommended that the agency refrain from procuring items/goods which are not included in the approved APP including approved changes thereto in compliance with Section 7.2 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the submission of copies of contracts and supporting documents to the Audit Team within five days after perfection thereof pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the LCE provide justification why the mandatory requirement for a CPES is not observed.  Henceforth, we enjoin LCE to revisit the provisions as provided under Section 13 of the Annex “E” of the R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR on the evaluation of contractor’s performance.  Further, that concerned department representatives be convened to discuss on the subject matter to ascertain commitment/s in terms of agency resources towards conformance to the mandatory requirement for a CPES and its necessary impact to the operations and structure of the LGU.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to invite at least two observers from a duly recognized private group in a sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand and from an NGO in every stage of procurement to enhance transparency of procurement process.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to ensure compliance and completeness of the documentary requirements as prescribed by the aforementioned laws and regulations and for the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of supporting documents before payment.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to deduct the 10% retention money from each progress payment for infrastructure projects up to 50% completion of the works, if the project is within the schedule otherwise, it should be continuously imposed.
Moreover, the total retention money shall be due for release only upon final acceptance of the project or upon the request of the contractor to substitute the same with an irrevocable standby letter of credit or surety bond callable on demand or other acceptable documents prescribed in Section 6.2, Annex E of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that the LCE direct the BAC to exercise care and caution in accepting and verifying bonds by bidders and contractors required in view of the procurement activity.  Further, require replacement of surety bonds issued by the surety company included in the Negative List for infrastructure projects the obligations/warranties insured of are still in force.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere with the submission of the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council and the Municipal Development Council pursuant to Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the proper charging of expenditures to the 30 percent Quick Response Fund and 70 percent Mitigation Fund to ensure that the funds are utilized in accordance with its intended purposes.

We recommended that Management maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF to cover all the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management in order to enhance the preparedness of the Municipality against disasters and avoid, lessen or limit their adverse impacts to the community. Further, for Management to include in the Annual Investment Plan/LDRRM Plan the programs/projects/ activities to be charged against the Special Trust Fund-LDRRMF.

We recommended that:
The Local School Board improve the planning, budgeting and utilization of the SEF budget, through the implementation of priority programs, projects and activities included in existing Joint Circulars of the DepEd, DBM and DILG on SEF; and
The LGU stop the payment of wages of ALS Instructional Managers as this is a proper charge to DepEd.

We recommended that Management improve practices in programming, prioritizing and implementing programs and projects to maximize the utilization of the Fund in attaining desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes for the benefit of the community.

We recommended that the agency identify specific individuals to represent the above-mentioned two sectors in full compliance with the required organizational structure of the SWMB under the RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the MENRO prepare the required reports on the performance status and analysis of implementation and enforcement of solid waste management and require all Poblacion Barangays to submit monitoring reports pursuant to Sangunian Bayan Resolution No. 132, series 2016.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Monthly Trial Balance and the Quarterly and Year-end Financial Statements within the prescribed timeframe in accordance with existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipality adhere to the mandatory posting on the GPPB Online Portal of information relative to Emergency Procurements under the Bayanihan Act, to ensure that accountability and transparency in the procurement is upheld.

We recommended Management direct the BAC to submit the lacking documentary requirements. Henceforth, strictly observe the documentary requirements to be submitted by supplier/contractor prior to the award of the contract to ensure that Management is only transacting with legally, financially and technically capable suppliers/contractors for Emergency Procurements under the Bayanihan Act.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Instruct the MDRRM Officer to submit to the Audit Team the Relief Distribution List of the recipients of the relief goods; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documentation to protect the interests of the government and to prevent unwarranted disbursements of public funds.

observations.recommendation:
We commended Management for its continuous effort to practice proper solid waste management, particularly, their formulation and updating of the 10-year SWMP, rehabilitation and maintenance of closed dumpsites and involvement of all sectors of the municipality in attaining their SWM objectives. 

We also recommended that the LGU speed up the implementation of the Alternative Solid Waste Processing Technology to efficiently manage the solid wastes generated within its jurisdiction and to reduce expenses from tipping fees paid to the private SLF contractor for the residual wastes of the LGU.

We recommended that Management:

Submit justification to avoid possible issuance of Notice of Disallowance with regard to the one million peso cost of Evacuation Center which was demolished and no longer existing;



Determine the proper legal action in order to protect itself, being the real party in interest in the case filed by the owner of the subject lot against Barangay Kinale; and



Henceforth, ascertain that lots are owned by the Municipality before constructing public structures to avoid wastage of public funds.
observations.managementAction:
Municipal Engineer submitted a letter justifying why she should not be held liable, because it is not her duty to determine ownership of the lot but merely implements the project.

No legal action was undertaken by the LGU as of this writing. However, as administrative case was heard by the Sanguniang Bayan regarding the falsification of documents pertaining to the Tax Declaration submitted as proof of ownership of the Barangay, imposing the sanction of reprimand to the PB and Barangay Council per SB Admin Case No. 001, s. 2019.

Management's commitment in ensuring that ownership of lots/properties are first established before implementing projects and constructing a structure are reflected in the projects completed  up to present.

Payment of Honoraria made to DepEd Personnel, other than those members of MBOC and PNP personnel who served and was on detail during the election, are not allowed to receive honoraria from LGU funds, since there is no legal basis, other than the reasonable meal allowances.

Job orders of the LGU who were required to work during the election are likewise not allowed to receive honoraria. They are to be paid based on their daily wage rate.

Recompute the correct amount to be paid to each employee, in a form of overtime pay for services rendered based on actual number of hours work, pursuant to CSC-DBM JC No. 1 s. 2015 dated November 25, 2015 subject to the availability of funds, PS ceiling limitations and the usual accounting, budgeting and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction:
COMELEC sent letter clarifying the payment for Honoraria made to the officials. COMELEC Minute Resolution No. 19-1054 promulgated October 30, 2019 in Re: In the Matter of the Study on the Grant of Honorarium or Allowance to the Members of the Board of Canvassers by LGUs.

In the said Resolution, Sangguniang Bayan, Sangguniang Panlungsod, Sangguniang Panlalawigan of municipalities, cities and provinces, respectively may continue to grant honorarium or allowance to the members of municipal/provincial board of canvassers for the May 13, 2019 National and Local Elections and all subsequent electoral exercises; such honorarium or allowance must not be simmilar to the honorarium or allowance being given by this Commission; and may provide in their respective ordinances that if the recipient already receiving a similar honorarium or allowance for the same service or period, respectively, for which payment is being claimed, the recipient may choose to receive whichever is higher but in no case must be the recipient receive both amounts.

In the said Resolution, Sangguniang bayan, sannguniang panlngsod, sannguniang panlalawigan of municipalites, cities and provinces, respectively may continue to grant an honorarium o allowance to the members of municipal/provincial board of canvassers for the May13, 2019 National and Local Elections and all subsequent electoral exercises; such honorarium or allowance must not be similar to the honorarium or allowance being given by this Commission; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Exert efforts to locate where the copies of the loan documents are kept. In case of inability to locate the LGU’s copies of the loan documents, Management make representations with the bank to request copies thereof for record purposes and to serve as bases in determining the amount of total annual amortization of the loan that should be classified as Current Liabilities; and
Thereafter, reclassify the Current portion of the Loans Payable-Domestic for a proper presentation and disclosure of the liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position as of the year-end.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to strictly monitor the progress done by the contractors of the delayed projects and inquire from them as to the reason/s for the delay in its completion. Impose penalties on the contractors for the delayed completion of the projects, if warranted;
Instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with each other in conducting feasibility studies and in planning the procurement process of future projects funded by the 20 percent EDF; and,
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to initiate the procurement process of developmental projects as soon as the funds become available and all documentary requirements have been complied with.

We also recommended that the LGU speed up the implementation of the Alternative Solid Waste Processing Technology to efficiently manage the solid wastes generated within its jurisdiction and to reduce expenses from tipping fees paid to the private SLF contractor for the residual wastes of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Supervise the LDRRM Office to achieve full implementation of the LDRRM PPAs to ensure that the expenses incurred are in accordance with the approved budget focusing on PPAs which require the highest priority in terms of urgency and impact on the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management; and
Maximize the utilization of the 70% Mitigation Fund and the STF to enhance the preparedness of the Municipality against disasters and avoid, lessen or limit their adverse impacts to the community.

We commended Management for implementing the LGSF-FA projects on time and recommended that it continue its practice of implementing projects within the agreed period so that the benefits to be derived therefrom may be enjoyed by the intended beneficiaries at the earliest time possible.

We recommended Management to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to hasten the preparation of the BRS and furnish a copy thereof to the Audit Team, together with the corresponding Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs), paid checks and original copies of Debit and Credit Memos for review and verification.
Make representation with the Bank to ensure prompt delivery of the Bank Statements. If the Banks Statements could not be released regularly by the Bank at the end of each month, the Municipal Accountant to secure snapshots of the Bank Statements to facilitate the preparation of the BRS; and
Take immediate remedial measures, such as augmenting the MAO with personnel from other departments, especially during report preparation and/or allowing them to render overtime services, subject to availability of funds, in order to update the submission of the BRS to meet mandated deadlines.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the LCE:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the last Quarter of CY 2019 Monthly Trial Balances and Year-End FS and, henceforth, to prepare and submit the Monthly Trial Balance and Quarterly Financial Statements, with complete Schedules and Annexes within the prescribed timeframe in accordance with existing rules and regulations;
Take appropriate immediate remedial measures, such as temporarily augmenting the MAO with personnel from other departments, especially during report preparation, and/or allowing them to render overtime, subject to the availability of funds, in order to ensure the timely preparation and submission of the financial statements and reports; and,
Allow qualified plantilla personnel of the MAO to attend trainings or seminars relevant to their job assignment, to equip them with the required knowledge and skills in order to improve their performance.

We recommended that Management intensify its efforts to collect RPT due from the concerned delinquent taxpayers.  If necessary, consider taking legal and administrative sanctions to effect collection thereof.

We recommended that Management:
a.	Submit justification to avoid possible issuance of Notice of Disallowance with regard to the one million peso cost of Evacuation Center which was demolished and no longer existing;
b.	Determine the proper legal action in order to protect itself, being the real party in interest in the case filed by the owner of the subject lot against Barangay Kinale; and
c.	Henceforth, ascertain that lots are owned by the Municipality before constructing public structures to avoid wastage of public funds.

We recommended that the Municipality continue implementing GAD PPAs to attain its objective of gender equality and contribute to the empowerment of its women constituents.  Further, continue updating and maintaining the GAD Database as an effective tool to aid Management in its decision-making.

We commended the MAO and recommend that it continue its diligence in the deduction of GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG Fund premium contributions and the remittance thereof, including the government shares, within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management require:

The Municipal Accountant, the Property/Supply Officer to reconcile the PPELC with the RPCPPE, respectively, and immediately effect necessary adjustments, if any;

The Municipal Engineer’s Office (MAO) to furnish the Accountant and the Property/Supply Officer of the Inventory Report of Buildings, School Buildings, Markets, Hospitals and Health Centres and Other Structures including Construction in Progress under their custody and records to facilitate the updating and reconciliation of these assets; and

The Supply Officer to prepare their RPCPPE by not just listing the properties found in a particular office but to classify these properties appropriately to facilitate easy reconciliation and validation processes.

officers (AOs) requesting immediate liquidation of the cash advance or
settlement of the amounts due from them;

letter and he/she fails to settle the cash advance or receivable, the Agency
Head shall cause the withholding of the payment of any money due the AO;
 The Municipal Accountant strictly require that the Cash Advances shall be
supported with approved payroll or estimate of expenses to support the
validity of the transaction, to avoid suspension in audit; and

officials and/or employees, prior to the liquidation of the previous ones.

auditing and budgeting laws, rules and regulations.

statements of the Agency.

to the usual accounting, auditing and budgeting laws, rules and regulations.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile and clean the
books of accounts, particularly the account Due to LGUs, in accordance with the
provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 in order to present fairly the financial
statements of the Agency.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Review the balances of payable accounts and identify the accounts with no
named claimants or creditors as well as supporting documents;
b. Verify accounts with identified creditors, through confirmation, whether
indeed they are the valid claimants or creditors and these are supported
with documents for said claims; and after which
c. Certify the outstanding payables, for which there are no valid
claimants/creditors and no supporting documents; and
i. Revert the verified amounts to the Government Equity for the General
and Special Education Funds by preparing the necessary Journal Entry
Voucher (JEV) as basis for recording in the books of accounts of the
Municipality; and
ii. As regards the TF accounts, return the unutilized balances to the
respective Source Agencies, unless otherwise stated in the MOA, in
which such amount may form part of the unappropriated surplus of the
LGU.

We commended Management for implementing the BUB projects and
programs on time and recommended to continue its practice of implementing
projects within the agreed period so that the benefits to be derived therefrom may be
enjoyed by the intended beneficiaries at the earliest time.

We recommended that the Head of the Procuring Agency (HOPE) require the
BAC Chairman or the BAC Secretariat Head in-charge in the submission of
Contract/Purchase Orders to the Audit Team for review and evaluation as well as
inspection to strictly comply with the aforesaid requirements relative to the
documentary requirements and timelines in order for the Audit Team to conduct
timely review and inspection. Otherwise, non-submission thereof may result in the
issuance of Notice of Suspension on said transaction for Contacts/POs not received by
the Auditor within the timelines set by the aforecited regulations.

We also recommended that Management require the concerned Official or
Employee in-Charge in the acceptance of deliveries to inform the Auditor of
deliveries within 24 hours upon delivery, in compliance with COA Circular No. 95-
006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommended that the Management require:
a. The Municipal Accountant to submit the Monthly Report of Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF for the months of April to December, 2018 for
monitoring purposes; and
b. Adhere strictly with the provision of Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002
dated September 12, 2012 and submit regularly to the COA Audit Team of the
Municipality the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of
each month through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management
Council and the Municipal Development Council.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Treasurer:
a. And other officials concerned to exert more efforts to collect the rentals
and business taxes from delinquent market stall occupants using the
remedies set forth in the Municipal Revenue Code and those included in
the internal policy of the Municipality, if necessary, in order to generate
additional income that will be used for the implementation of programs,
projects and activities of the Municipality; and
b. To organize and update her records, at the same time, periodically provide
the Task Force Market/Market Administrator of the list of delinquent
stallholders, both for market rentals and business taxes, and amount still
due and demandable for monitoring purposes, and strictly enforce the
Revenue Code provisions relative to the automatic closure upon non-
renewal of business permit or non-payment of taxes and fees due to the
Municipality.

We recommended that Management:
a. Provide an ultimatum to the delinquent market stallholders to pay the
unpaid balance of business taxes and rental fees to the Municipality, within
a fixed grace period;
b. In case the delinquent market stallholders are in default of the first, for the
Municipal Mayor, as authorized by the Municipality’s Revenue Code, to
order the closure of said businesses for being operated illegally; and
c. Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer shall provide the Licensing Office the
list of delinquent business owners before the ensuing year commences, in
order for the latter to approve the issuance of Mayor’s Permit to those who
have no outstanding liability from the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. The Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant with the
Final List of Delinquent Market Stallholders with amount due and
penalties for both business taxes and market rentals before the end of each
year to facilitate timely recording of accrued rental income and business
taxes; and
b. The Municipal Accountant to recognize the total unpaid rentals, business
taxes, surcharges and interests as of December 31, 2018 in the books of the
Municipality to the appropriate receivable and income/equity account, in
compliance with theprovisions of PPSAS 13, in order to fairly present the
financial statements of the Municipality. Henceforth, recognize these
revenues by accrual method.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
a. Intensify the tax collection campaign to maximize collection of RPT due to
the Municipal Government in order to increase its revenue and have extra
funds available to finance the delivery of basic services and facilities; and
b. Send demand letters to collect taxes from delinquent taxpayers and in case
of failure to collect, avail of the remedies through levy on real property or
judicial actions provided under the Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended that Management:
a. Maximize the utilization of the 20% LDF by the conduct of proper
planning and coordination among the concerned officials so that all
programmed development projects during the year could be fully
implemented for the benefit of the constituents; and
b. Direct the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator to properly
monitor and evaluate the implementation of projects and submit periodic
reports to the Office of the Municipal Mayor incorporating issues and
concerns affecting the execution of projects or create a Project Monitoring
Committee to do the said tasks as a team.

We recommended that the Management:
a.) Regularly monitor the closed dumpsites and ensure that the procedures
for its closure conform with the provisions of the SCRPs of the
Municipality in accordance with R.A. 9003 and its IRR, so that further
pollution may be prevented and the safety of the constituents will be
guaranteed;
b.) Allocate sufficient funds to make the necessary repairs to the Closed
Dumpsite in Brgy. Barobo; and
c.) Follow up with the DENR MGB the result of the assessment that it
conducted to the proposed location of Sanitary Landfill in Brgy. Lanigay
so that upon favorable outcome, the construction of the Sanitary Landfill
may be initiated at the earliest possible time and the costly contract with a
third party to provide services for the disposal of solid waste will no
longer be necessary.

We commended the Municipal Accounting Office and recommend to continue
its diligence in the deduction of GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG Fund premium
contributions and the remittance thereof, including the government shares within
the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Assess the necessity of the services of consultants to avoid unnecessary
disbursement of public funds and evaluate whether the responsibilities of
the existing consultants are duplicate of those of the regular employees;
b. Limit the hiring of consultants to urgent and critical work assignments
that are highly technical in nature and require expertise that is not
available in-house for the period of six months only, renewable based on
the necessity of their services;
c. Stop contracting with private lawyers to avoid unnecessary expenditures
outlined under COA Circular No. 98-002, and instead hire a qualified
legal officer, if really necessary, considering budgetary constraints; and
c. Provide justification or legal basis on the employment of these consultants
in order that the amount paid for professional fees will not be disallowed in
audit.

We recommended that Management create a policy which will be used in the
employment of job orders and submit the same to the Sanggunian for approval. We
also recommend that Management revisit the provisions/stipulations of the Job Order
contract and see to it that both the parties in the contract are protected.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal HRMO to observe strictly the required employee’s monthly minimum net
99
take home pay provided for under the pertinent provision of the General Provisions
of the Annual GAA of 2018.

Direct the Bids and Awards Committee particularly the BAC Secretariat to document the transactions and/or events as it transpired during the different phases of the procurement process to support its validity and propriety; 
Direct the Municipal Accountant to strengthen the processing/pre-audit activities, as part of the Agency’s overall accounting and internal control system, avoid control overrides, and ensure that transactions processed, paid and recorded are duly approved and supported with complete documentation and/or technical requirements. The use of Checklist provided for under the Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and the Revised RIRR of RA 9184 may prove useful as guide in the processing of claims for payment; and
Require the BAC, GSO and OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking supporting documents as listed in Annex F, data and/or information for our review, otherwise the related transactions may be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: There are still some recurring transactions that are being processed which are urgent in nature such as during calamites were control overrides occur.
observations.reason: There are still some recurring transactions that are being processed which are urgent in nature such as during calamites were control overrides occur.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan of the Municipality for the establishment of the IAU/IAS as required under AO No. 278 and 70, in order to improve and strengthen the Internal Control System of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Ms. Lorena Bobis, CPA was hired last September 2018 as Management Internal Audit Analyst to handle implementation and maintenance of Internal Control System.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to hasten the preparation and submission of the aforesaid Monthly CRAAF for CY 2016,together with the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) of Individual Accountable Officers and henceforth to submit the same to the Audit Team for verification, within the mandated deadlines.

On-going | Engr. Alfred Mirabueno qualified for the position of Environmental Management Specialist II was hired as Consultant to implement programs of Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan. He was hired as permanent employee last March 1, 2019. Job orders were also been contracted in 2018 to effectively and efficiently implement the ESWMP programs. A 10- year plan was also submitted to the  EMB and Province for the updated 10 year plan in accordance with the requirements of the EMB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineer to make a study in order to come-up with an acceptable standard ratio between materials and labor components for every class of Infrastructure projects implemented by Administration after considering the proposed project site or location, topography and actual field and soil condition.  Consider the procurement of the labor component thru Pakyaw Contract instead of thru Job Order basis for a simpler way of supervision and accountability.
observations.managementAction: On-going | The Office of the Engineer is following a standard DUPA (Detailed Unit Cost Analysis) standards provided by the DPWH with the consideration of “Hauling Cost Analysis” of the materials and detailed estimates of the project.  The LGU is adopting, in most cases, the “By Administration” mode of implementation  because it lessens some indirect costs such as profit and OCM that are usually incorporated in “By Contract” mode.  This resulted to longer or bigger scope in terms of accomplishment as compared to the regular By Contract mode.
observations.reason: On-going

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Human Resource and Management Officer to discuss and make the necessary arrangement with the Agency’s GSB for the use of ATM in the payment of salaries, wages and other benefits of the officials and employees of the LGU, so that government funds may be safeguarded against loss or misuse and to ensure efficiency and economy in its cash management.
observations.managementAction:
Completed with regards to the submission of requirements needed to make the necessary arrangements with Agency GSB for the use of ATM in the payment of salaries wages and Other benefits.

As of Jan 2019, salaries of the employees were paid through ATM (LBP)

observations.reason: There are loans which have been applied in prior years that are not yet matured, hence, the salary deduction is still the same thus, the net take home pay remains the same.
